source_limitations: Analysis limited to local dependency clusters and class-conditioned variation without global aggregation or cross-feature group synthesis.
Analysis limited to local neighborhoods and single-feature shortcuts without global aggregation.
Direct distribution verification for features e11 and e12 was not performed due to step budget exhaustion, though strong locality and shared group context support inferred similarity.

findings: Average Packet Size (e8) shows moderate variance ratio and unique value disparity with high class separation between BENIGN and DDoS classes.

Backward packet length features (e9 through e12) exhibit strong class-conditioned structural variation with high variance ratios and significantly higher unique values in BENIGN compared to DDoS, confirmed by high class separation signals in verified features.
limitations: Direct distribution verification for features e11 and e12 was not performed due to step budget exhaustion, though strong locality and shared group context support inferred similarity.

findings: Features e13 through e22 exhibit strong dependency concentration with near-perfect linear correlations, indicating structural redundancy.
Class-conditioned variation in features e8 through e12 shows high variance ratios and unique value disparities between BENIGN and DDoS classes.

Dependency patterns in e13-e22 align with and likely reflect the class-conditioned structural variation observed in e8-e12, suggesting interplay between dependency concentration and class-conditioned variation.
limitations: Analysis limited to local dependency clusters and class-conditioned variation without global aggregation or cross-feature group synthesis.

findings: Moderate label neighborhood conflicts and duplicate label conflicts detected in backward packet length features indicate unstable feature importance.
Moderate shortcut signal found in Bwd Packet Length Max suggests shortcut-like predictive leverage.
Strong linear dependencies among features e8 through e22 reveal entanglement between class-conditioned variation and redundancy, contributing to selection bias.
limitations: Analysis limited to local neighborhoods and single-feature shortcuts without global aggregation.
